flag: klo 9–17 could mean inclusive range of 9 hours (fires at :00 each) or exclusive end; Finnish readers generally read this as 9:00 through 17:00 inclusive, which is fine, but the boundary at 17 is slightly ambiguous (does it fire at 17:00?)
"0–29 minuutin kohdalla" is grammatically clear but unusual phrasing; a reader might momentarily parse it as a duration rather than minute positions 0 through 29
cron typically has 1-minute minimum resolution; "30 sekunnin välein" presupposes second-level cron, which is non-standard and may confuse readers expecting standard cron

"5 ja 10 sekunnin kohdalla, maanantaisin klo 9.30" — the comma junction leaves the scoping of "maanantaisin klo 9.30" ambiguous: does it restrict the whole expression or only the second arm? Also mixes second-level and time-of-day constraints in one clause without a clear connective

"15 sekunnin välein, 30 minuutin kohdalla klo 9.30–17.30 maanantaista perjantaihin" — multiple comma-separated constraints stack without explicit conjunctions; unclear whether "30 minuutin kohdalla" restricts when the 15-second interval fires or is a separate condition; heavy parsing load
"klo 9.30.15" uses a third dot-separated component for seconds; Finnish time notation uses dots for HH.MM but not for seconds — a reader is likely to be baffled or misread this as a typo

"klo 0.09.45 ja 0.17.45" — three-part time with seconds encoded as HH.MM.SS is non-standard in Finnish; "0.09" looks like 9 minutes past midnight, making "0.09.45" read as midnight + 9 min + 45 sec, which is parseable but very unusual notation

"0–30 minuutin kohdalla klo 9 sekä klo 17–19" — the shared minute spec "0–30" could scope only klo 9 or both klo 9 and the range 17–19; "sekä" is additive but does not clarify whether minute range carries over to 17–19
"klo 9–20 ja 22" — the isolated "22" after a range looks like it extends the range (9–22) to some readers; the intended reading (range 9–20 plus standalone hour 22) requires a second look

"arkipäivänä lähinnä kuukauden 15. päivää kesäkuussa" is precise but complex; "lähinnä" (nearest) is clear to a Finnish reader, though the compound logic (nearest weekday to 15th, in June) requires careful parsing
"1–59 minuutin kohdalla" — firing every minute except :00 is an unusual schedule; the description is accurate but a reader may wonder if :00 exclusion is intentional or an error
"1 minuutin ja 1 sekunnin kohdalla" is unambiguous but somewhat verbose; minor cognitive load from mixing minute and second granularity in one phrase

"joulukuussa klo 23.59 joko 31. päivänä tai perjantaisin" — the shared month+time prefix "joulukuussa klo 23.59" preceding "joko…tai" is syntactically ambiguous: does "joulukuussa" scope both arms of the disjunction, or only the first? Finnish grammar places the shared prefix before the disjunction, so a careful reader will accept it, but the scoping of "klo 23.59" over the perjantai arm is not entirely obvious

"0–30 minuutin kohdalla joka toinen tunti" is clear in intent; a reader may briefly wonder whether "joka toinen tunti" means even hours, odd hours, or simply alternating from some anchor, but the phrasing is acceptable
